hero.subtitle: Dropply encrypts your files and text right in the browser — with a password or a public key. Share the finished ciphertext with a code; the link never carries the key.
hero.trust: No accounts · No tracking · Open source

features.title: Privacy by design, not by promise
features.subtitle: Strong cryptography runs entirely on your device — no server, no account, no trust required.

local.title: Local encryption
local.desc: Encrypt or decrypt any file or text in your browser. Nothing is uploaded.

keys.title: Password or public key
keys.desc: Lock with a password, or encrypt to someone's public key so only they can open it.

share.title: Zero-knowledge sharing
share.desc: Upload the finished ciphertext and share a code. The link carries no key.

private.title: No account, no tracking
private.desc: No sign-up, no logins, no analytics — just the tool.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.subtitle: Everything you need to know about keys, encryption, and privacy.

create.q: Where do I create a key pair?
create.a: Open Keys in the header → Your keys → Create. A 12-word recovery phrase derives your key pair; only the phrase is stored, on this device.

encryptFile.q: How do I encrypt a file?
encryptFile.a: Pick File, choose Password or Public key, drop a file, enter the key, and click Encrypt.

decryptFile.q: How do I decrypt a file I received?
decryptFile.a: Drop the encrypted file into the tool — it detects the format and switches to Decrypt. Enter the password or your private key.

encryptText.a: Yes — switch to Message and type or paste. The result can be copied or downloaded.

share.a: Encrypt it, then click Share on the result card. You get a 6-character code and link; the recipient uses Retrieve.

uploaded.a: Only when you Share. Local encrypt and decrypt never upload. A shared item is ciphertext only.

canRead.q: Can you read my keys or files?
canRead.a: No. Keys stay in your browser and the server only ever stores ciphertext — it's zero-knowledge.

forgotPin.q: What if I forget my PIN?
forgotPin.a: The PIN only locks the key-manager UI. If you forget it you can reset it, which wipes all saved keys on this device.

footer.tagline: Client-side encryption and zero-knowledge sharing. Your keys never leave your browser.
footer.trust: No accounts · No tracking · Open source

keys.title: Key manager
keys.description: Generate a key pair, save recipients, and set a PIN. Everything stays on this device.

lock.resetDesc: This deletes all saved keys and the PIN from this device. It cannot be undone.

owner.mnemonicPlaceholder: Enter or generate a 12-word recovery phrase
owner.deleteDesc: The recovery phrase will be removed from this device.

receiver.deleteDesc: This public key will be removed from this device.

password.everyTime: The PIN locks the key manager each time it opens. It does not encrypt your stored keys.

keys.privateKeyLabel: Your private key or recovery phrase
keys.publicKeyPlaceholder: Base58 public key, or pick a saved recipient

error.title: Oh no!
error.message: There was an issue with our storefront. This could be a temporary issue, please try your action again.

notFound.title: Something's missing
notFound.message: Sorry, the page you are looking for doesn't exist or has been moved.

email.title: Share via Email
email.sentMessage: The retrieval code has been shared successfully.
email.messageHint: This message will be included in the email
email.securityNote: The email contains only the retrieval code. The recipient still needs the decryption password or their private key to read the content.

input.decryptDescription: Decrypt files and text locally in your browser.

input.messagePlaceholderDecrypt: Paste the ciphertext you want to decrypt

results.title: Results
results.emptyDescription: Encrypted and decrypted items will appear here.
results.emptyHint: Everything runs locally in your browser.
results.historyDesc: Your recent encrypt/decrypt results (this browser only).

dialog.decryptedSuccess: Your text has been decrypted locally.

share.title: Share this result
share.desc: The finished ciphertext is uploaded as-is. The link carries no key — the recipient decrypts it with the password or their own private key.
